finding.next_action: Compile exhaustive list of active SIDs/BIDs by reviewing county ordinance appendices, clerk records, and recent bills for all four counties (focus Maui/Kauai for any active ones); locate any parcel or boundary GIS layers or maps from district management associations or county finance/tax offices; confirm no statewide registry via DCCA, DBEDT, or AG.
finding.notes: Statewide enabling authority is HRS §46-80.5 (Special improvement district), allowing charter counties to enact ordinances creating SIDs for supplemental maintenance/security, environmental work, natural resources/hazards/climate adaptation, and other services/improvements that restore or promote business activity, with special assessments and optional bonds/association management (nonprofit under Ch. 414D). These are the legal equivalent of BIDs; Honolulu ROH Ch. 28 and Hawaii County Code Ch. 35 implement them (explicitly calling some BIDs); Maui Ord. 4382 (2016) adds enabling chapter for SIDs. Known active examples (Honolulu-centric): Waikiki SID/BID No. 1 (est. ~2000), Fort Street Mall SID No. 2 (expanded 2025 via Bill 51/Ord. to Downtown Honolulu BID), Waikiki Beach SID (2015). Hawaii County has Kailua Village BID under Ch. 35. Distinguish from: traditional improvement districts (HRS 46-80), community facilities districts (HRS 46-80.1), tax increment districts, zoning/design special districts (e.g., Honolulu zoning special districts GIS – different purpose), and HCDA improvement districts. No statewide registry, comprehensive official list, or unified GIS of BID/SID boundaries located; districts are local ordinances. Coverage is partial (authoritative enabling + major local codes + known district sites verified; full inventory of all active districts and boundaries incomplete). Failed or sparse searches for Kauai active districts and full statewide maps do not prove absence.
